content:
# Joshua Chapter 23 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pages 247-248 | **Source Image:** `../../source-texts/hebrew-OT/JOS_023_page_001.jpg`

---

**1** And it was, from many days after that YHWH had given rest to Israel from all their enemies round about, and Joshua was old, advanced in days.

**2** And Joshua called to all Israel, to their elders, and to their heads, and to their judges, and to their officers, and he said to them, "I am old, I have advanced in days.

**3** And you, you have seen all that YHWH your God has done to all these nations from before you, for YHWH your God, he [is] the one fighting for you.

**4** See, I have allotted to you these nations, the remaining [ones], [as] an inheritance for your tribes, from the Jordan, and all the nations that I have cut off, and the Great Sea [toward] the setting of the sun.

**5** And YHWH your God, he shall thrust them out from before you, and he shall drive them out from before you, and you shall possess their land, as YHWH your God spoke to you.

**6** And you shall be very strong to keep and to do all that is written in the scroll of the Torah of Moses, so as not to turn from it [to the] right or [to the] left,

**7** so as not to come among these nations, these remaining [ones] with you; and the name of their gods you shall not bring to remembrance, and you shall not swear [by them], and you shall not serve them, and you shall not bow down to them.

**8** But to YHWH your God you shall cling, as you have done until this day.

**9** And YHWH has driven out from before you nations great and mighty, and you — no man has stood before you until this day.

**10** One man of you pursues a thousand, for YHWH your God, he [is] the one fighting for you, as he spoke to you.

**11** And you shall guard your souls very [much] to love YHWH your God.

**12** For if you shall indeed turn back, and you cling to the remnant of these nations, these remaining [ones] with you, and you intermarry with them, and you come among them, and they among you,

**13** knowing, you shall know that YHWH your God will not continue to drive out these nations from before you; and they shall be to you a snare and a trap, and a scourge on your sides, and thorns in your eyes, until you perish from upon this good land which YHWH your God has given to you.

**14** And behold, I [am] going today in the way of all the earth; and you shall know with all your heart and with all your soul that not one word has fallen from all the good words which YHWH your God spoke concerning you; all have come to you, not one word from them has fallen.

**15** And it shall be, as all the good word has come upon you which YHWH your God spoke to you, so YHWH shall bring upon you all the evil word, until he has destroyed you from upon this good land which YHWH your God has given to you.

**16** When you transgress the covenant of YHWH your God which he commanded you, and you go and serve other gods, and you bow down to them, then the anger of YHWH shall burn against you, and you shall perish quickly from upon the good land which he has given to you."

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v1 — "advanced in days"** (בָּא בַּיָּמִים): Literally "come in the days." This is a standard Hebrew idiom for old age, retained literally.

2. **v4 — "the Great Sea toward the setting of the sun"** (הַיָּם הַגָּדוֹל מְבוֹא הַשָּׁמֶשׁ): "Great Sea" is the Mediterranean. מְבוֹא הַשָּׁמֶשׁ literally "the entrance of the sun," i.e., sunset/west.

3. **v6 — "scroll of the Torah of Moses"** (סֵפֶר תּוֹרַת מֹשֶׁה): סֵפֶר rendered "scroll" rather than "book" to reflect the original medium.

4. **v7 — "bring to remembrance"** (תַזְכִּירוּ): Hiphil of זכר — to cause to be remembered, i.e., to invoke or mention. The prohibition is against even speaking the names of foreign gods.

5. **v10 — "One man of you pursues a thousand"**: אִישׁ אֶחָד מִכֶּם יִרְדָּף אָלֶף — The verb is imperfect, expressing ongoing capacity granted by YHWH.

6. **v11 — "guard your souls very [much]"** (וְנִשְׁמַרְתֶּם מְאֹד לְנַפְשֹׁתֵיכֶם): נֶפֶשׁ here carries the reflexive sense — "guard yourselves exceedingly."

7. **v12 — "if you shall indeed turn back"** (כִּי אִם שׁוֹב תָּשׁוּבוּ): Infinitive absolute construction (שׁוֹב תָּשׁוּבוּ) intensifying the turning away — "if turning, you turn back."

8. **v13 — "knowing, you shall know"** (יָדוֹעַ תֵּדְעוּ): Infinitive absolute construction preserving the emphatic doubling of ידע.

9. **v13 — "scourge on your sides"** (שֹׁטֵט בְּצִדֵּיכֶם): שׁוֹטֵט — a whip or scourge. The imagery is of painful affliction on the flanks, paired with "thorns in your eyes."

10. **v14 — "in the way of all the earth"** (בְּדֶרֶךְ כָּל הָאָרֶץ): A euphemism for dying — "the path all the earth [goes]."

11. **v15 — "all the evil word"** (כָּל הַדָּבָר הָרָע): Parallel to "all the good word" — דָּבָר here means "thing" or "matter" as much as "word," but EMET retains the literal "word" to preserve the parallelism with verse 14.

12. **v16 — "the anger of YHWH shall burn"** (וְחָרָה אַף יְהוָה): Literally "and the nose/anger of YHWH shall burn hot." אַף is "nose" — anger expressed as the flaring of nostrils.

13. **YHWH** (יהוה): The divine name is transliterated throughout, never replaced with "the LORD" or "God," in keeping with EMET's commitment to the text as written.
